query: Florida 2026E special session budget HB 5001-E appropriation total May 2026

# Florida 2026E Special Session Budget — HB 5001-E Research Report

## Key Finding: Total Appropriation

**HB 5001-E (General Appropriations Act) carries a total appropriation of $113,575,226,021** (~$113.6 billion) as filed. This figure is listed on the official Florida Senate bill page for the 2026E special session.

## Bill Overview

- **Bill Number:** HB 5001-E
- **Full Title:** General Appropriations Act
- **Session:** 2026E (Special Session E)
- **Sponsor:** Representative McClure (House)
- **Companion Bill:** SB 2500-E, sponsored by Senator Hooper (Senate), with an appropriation of **$115,025,780,033** (~$115.0 billion)
- **Implementing Bill:** HB 5003-E — "Implementing the 2026-2027 General Appropriations Act"
- **Fiscal Year Covered:** July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027, plus supplemental appropriations for the period ending June 30, 2026
- **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026 (or upon becoming law, whichever is later; retroactive to July 1, 2026 if enacted after that date)

## Legislative History (as of May 20, 2026)

| Date | Chamber | Action |
|------|---------|--------|
| May 5, 2026 | House | Filed; referred to House Calendar |
| May 12, 2026 | House | Read 1st, 2nd, 3rd time; **Passed YEAS 98, NAYS 8**; requested Senate pass or agree to Budget Conference |
| May 12, 2026 | Senate | Received; substituted for SB 2500-E; amendments adopted; **Passed as amended YEAS 32, NAYS 0**; acceded to conference request |
| May 12, 2026 | Both | **Bill placed in Budget Conference**; Conference Committee appointed |

## Current Status

As of May 12, 2026 (the last recorded action), **HB 5001-E is currently in Budget Conference**. The Senate passed an amended version (with the Senate's $115.0 billion offer vs. the House's $113.6 billion offer), and a conference committee has been appointed across multiple subcommittees:

- **Budget (At Large):** Chair Sen. Hooper
- **Agriculture, Environment & General Government:** Chair Sen. Brodeur
- **Criminal and Civil Justice:** Chair Sen. Garcia
- **Health and Human Services:** Chair Sen. Trumbull
- **Higher Education:** Chair Sen. Harrell
- **Pre-K – 12 Education:** Chair Sen. Burgess
- **Transportation, Tourism & Economic Development:** Chair Sen. DiCeglie

The conference committee must reconcile the ~$1.45 billion gap between the House ($113.6B) and Senate ($115.0B) versions.

## Comparison: House vs. Senate Offers

| Version | Total Appropriation |
|---------|-------------------|
| HB 5001-E (House, as filed) | $113,575,226,021 |
| SB 2500-E (Senate) | $115,025,780,033 |
| **Difference** | **$1,450,554,012** |

## Context

- The bill was filed during the **2026E Special Session** — notably the fifth special session designation of 2026 (sessions A through E have been designated, with 2026, 2026D, and 2026E visible in the legislative session list).
- Budget conference negotiations were actively underway as of mid-May 2026, with Florida Politics reporting multiple line-item disagreements between the House and Senate on specific appropriations.
- The final conference report has **not yet been adopted** as of the latest data (May 12, 2026 last action).

## Sources

1. **Florida Senate — HB 5001-E Bill Page:** https://www.flsenate.gov/Session/Bill/2026E/5001 (accessed May 20, 2026)
2. **Florida Senate — SB 2500-E Bill Page:** https://www.flsenate.gov/Session/Bill/2026E/2500 (accessed May 20, 2026)
3. **Florida Politics — Budget Conference Coverage:** https://floridapolitics.com (accessed May 20, 2026)

The Senate companion bill SB 2500-E carries a higher total appropriation of $115,025,780,033 (~$115.0 billion)

The House passed HB 5001-E on May 12, 2026 with a vote of 98 YEAS to 8 NAYS
The Senate passed an amended version on May 12, 2026 with a vote of 32 YEAS to 0 NAYS

The bill covers FY 2026-2027 (July 1, 2026 – June 30, 2027) plus supplemental appropriations for the period ending June 30, 2026
